regrade_requests.student_comment:
I think I should get +3 points for rubric item Runtime Analysis 
since I mentioned that solving each subproblem takes atleast O(n) time like the solution.
regrade_requests.staff_comment: The solution indicates that the total runtime is O(n), not O(n) for each subproblem

regrade_requests.student_comment:
I think I should get +9 points for rubic item "Divide the problem into three subproblems: a, 10^n/2, and b as stated in the question and recurse on all three subproblems".
This is because I mention in the final solution that you need to convert a, b, and 10^n/2 to binary recursively in the recursive case.

rubric_items.description: Correctly runs algorithm until the queue is completely empty (not just returning the first time $$t$$ is seen) and returns $$d(t)$$.
_(Partial)_ Correctly isolates the $$(u, v)$$ edge to apply the $$-1$$ weight, while correctly processing all other edges with standard $$+1$$ BFS logic.

_(Partial)_ Explicitly modifies the BFS queueing rule to check if $$d(u) - 1 < d(v)$$ , and if so, explicitly states that $$v$$ must be pushed **back** into the queue (or the queue is cleared and reset to $$v$$) to update its descendants.
_(Partial)_ Specifies running BFS from $$s$$ in Layer 0, and attempts to extract the shortest path by comparing $$d(t_0)$$ and $$d(t_1)$$. (Allow for minor mathematical errors here).
Shows why the adjustment (subtracting 1 or 2) yields the correct final path weight.
Mentions making two copies of the graph.
_(Partial)_ Attempts to justify the math behind their specific path combination or layer adjustment, but leaves a minor gap in their logical proof.
_(Partial)_  Construction of the duplicated graph: states there are two layers AND correctly define the directed cross-edge from $$u$$ in Layer 0 to $$v$$ in Layer 1.
Correctly returns the minimum of $$d(s, t)$$ and $$d(s, u) + d(v, t) - 1$$.
_(Partial)_ Outlines the three necessary BFS runs and attempts to combine the paths to find the minimum and correct for the $$-1$$ edge. (Allow for minor mathematical errors here).
_(Partial)_ Identifies the two binary cases (the shortest path either uses the unique edge exactly once or not at all)

_(Partial)_ Breaks problem into three distinct BFS runs on a graph without the $$-1$$ edge: one from $$s$$ to $$t$$, one from $$s$ to $u$$, and one from $$v$$ to $$t$$. (Allow for minor logical errors here).
Stated algorithm is slower than $$O(|V| + |E|)$$  time. (Don't award any other points).
[Partial] Greedily takes min($$q_i$$, remaining space) for each mineral in order
[Partial] Sorts the minerals in decreasing order of value
[Partial] Claims that the total value is either maintained or reduced with that change
[Partial] Shows that an "optimal solution" which diverges from the greedy solution replaces a high-valued mineral for a lower-valued mineral
[Partial] Correct number of subproblems with explanation
Divide the problem into three subproblems: $$a$$, $$10^{n/2}$$, and $$b$$ as stated in the question and recurse on all three subproblems.
[Tree Method] Argue that each subproblem of size n/2 is doing $$O(n^{\log_2 3}/3)$$ work, and it has 3 subproblems. So the work done on that level is $$O(n^{\log_2 3})$$
Use Karatsuba algorithm to multiply $$a$$ and $$10^{n/2}$$, then add $$b$$ to the product to get the result.
Correctly apply the master theorem to get the runtime
[Partial] Attempted to divide the problem into subproblems, for example, recursing on $$a$$ and $$b$$ but not $$10^{n/2}$$.
[Partial] Correctly argue that Karatsuba dominates the extra workload and it is $$O(n^{\log_2 3})$$
[Tree Method]  Argue that there are $$\log n$$ levels
[Tree Method] Conclude that the runtime is the number of levels times the work for every level, which gives $$O(n^{\log_2 3} \log n)$$
State the base case: when $$n$$ is 1, directly return the binary representation of the number
[Partial] Attempts to divide the problem into 2 subproblems AND handles the $$10^{n/2}$$ case explicitly with potentially mistakes.
[Partial] Considers selecting the last job and not selecting the last job
[Partial] Found the correct runtime per subproblem
[Partial] Finds the latest non-overlapping job if selecting the last job
[Partial] Found the correct number of subproblems

rubric_item_groups.description: Base case (only awarded if credit was given for subproblem)
Runtime analysis (only awarded if credit was given for recurrence)
